messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following cloud accounting software privacy policy for GDPR compliance:

"AccountCloud processes financial transaction data, payroll records, and tax filings on behalf of your business. We act as a data processor and you remain the data controller. Processing occurs only on your instructions as set out in our Terms of Service. We use AWS and Azure infrastructure, both of which operate certified EU data centres. Data is retained in accordance with your instructions or applicable legal requirements. We assist you in fulfilling data subject requests on reasonable notice. Our DPO is at dpo@accountcloud.com. We will notify you of any confirmed breach within 24 hours. Sub-processors are listed at accountcloud.com/subprocessors and updated with 30 days notice."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Correctly structured as processor. Lawful basis responsibility sits with the customer as controller. Processing on instructions per Art. 28.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Payroll data may include health-related information (sick pay, disability adjustments). No explicit acknowledgement or additional safeguards for special category data within payroll are mentioned.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Processing limited to customer instructions.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Processing scope determined by customer instructions.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] Retention per customer instructions or legal requirements is an appropriate processor-level statement.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PASS] Commitment to assist with data subject requests on reasonable notice satisfies Art. 28(3)(e).

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] Sub-processors (AWS, Azure) named with EU data centre confirmation. Full sub-processor list maintained at a URL with 30-day notice of changes.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] EU data centres confirmed for both AWS and Azure. No transfer concerns.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PASS] DPO disclosed at dpo@accountcloud.com.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] 24-hour customer notification stated, exceeding the Art. 33 minimum.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 9 (payroll special category data risk not addressed)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Add explicit provisions for handling special category data within payroll (e.g., sick pay, disability-related adjustments) including enhanced access controls and processing restrictions
